book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
The First Theological Oration. A Preliminary Discourse Against the Eunomians.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: The First Theological Oration. A Preliminary Discourse Against the Eunomians.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The First Theological Oration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A Preliminary Discourse Against the Eunomians.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am to speak against persons who pride themselves on their eloquence; so, to begin with a text of Scripture, “Behold, I am against thee, O thou proud one,” not only in thy system of teaching, but also in thy hearing, and in thy tone of mind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For there are certain persons who have not only their ears and their tongues, but even, as I now perceive, their hands too, itching for our words; who delight in profane babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called, and strifes about words, which tend to no profit; for so Paul, the Preacher and Establisher of the “Word cut short,” the disciple and teacher of the Fishermen, calls all that is excessive or superfluous in discourse.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But as to those to whom we refer, would that they, whose tongue is so voluble and clever in applying itself to noble and approved language, would likewise pay some attention to actions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For then perhaps in a little while they would become less sophistical, and less absurd and strange acrobats of words, if I may use a ridiculous expression about a ridiculous subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since they neglect every path of righteousness, and look only to this one point, namely, which of the propositions submitted to them they shall bind or loose, (like those persons who in the theatres perform wrestling matches in public, but not that kind of wrestling in which the victory is won according to the rules of the sport, but a kind to deceive the eyes of those who are ignorant in such matters, and to catch applause), and every marketplace must buzz with their talking; and every dinner party be worried to death with silly talk and boredom; and every festival be made unfestive and full of dejection, and every occasion of mourning be consoled by a greater calamity—their questions—and all the women’s apartments accustomed to simplicity be thrown into confusion and be robbed of its flower of modesty by the torrent of their words…since, I say this is so, the evil is intolerable and not to be borne, and our Great Mystery is in danger of being made a thing of little moment.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well then, let these spies bear with us, moved as we are with fatherly compassion, and as holy Jeremiah says, torn in our hearts; let them bear with us so far as not to give a savage reception to our discourse upon this subject; and let them, if indeed they can, restrain their tongues for a short while and lend us their ears.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: However that may be, you shall at any rate suffer no loss.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For either we shall have spoken in the ears of them that will hear, and our words will bear some fruit, namely an advantage to you (since the Sower soweth the Word upon every kind of mind; and the good and fertile bears fruit), or else you will depart despising this discourse of ours as you have despised others, and having drawn from it further material for gainsaying and railing at us, upon which to feast yourselves yet more.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And you must not be astonished if I speak a language which is strange to you and contrary to your custom, who profess to know everything and to teach everything in a too impetuous and generous manner…not to pain you by saying ignorant and rash.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not to every one, my friends, does it belong to philosophize about God; not to every one; the Subject is not so cheap and low; and I will add, not before every audience, nor at all times, nor on all points; but on certain occasions, and before certain persons, and within certain limits.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not to all men, because it is permitted only to those who have been examined, and are passed masters in meditation, and who have been previously purified in soul and body, or at the very least are being purified.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the impure to touch the pure is, we may safely say, not safe, just as it is unsafe to fix weak eyes upon the sun’s rays.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what is the permitted occasion?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is when we are free from all external defilement or disturbance, and when that which rules within us is not confused with vexatious or erring images; like persons mixing up good writing with bad, or filth with the sweet odours of unguents.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is necessary to be truly at leisure to know God; and when we can get a convenient season, to discern the straight road of the things divine.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And who are the permitted persons?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: They to whom the subject is of real concern, and not they who make it a matter of pleasant gossip, like any other thing, after the races, or the theatre, or a concert, or a dinner, or still lower employments.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To such men as these, idle jests and pretty contradictions about these subjects are a part of their amusement.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Next, on what subjects and to what extent may we philosophize?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: On matters within our reach, and to such an extent as the mental power and grasp of our audience may extend.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: No further, lest, as excessively loud sounds injure the hearing, or excess of food the body, or, if you will, as excessive burdens beyond the strength injure those who bear them, or excessive rains the earth; so these too, being pressed down and overweighted by the stiffness, if I may use the expression, of the arguments should suffer loss even in respect of the strength they originally possessed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now, I am not saying that it is not needful to remember God at all times;…I must not be misunderstood, or I shall be having these nimble and quick people down upon me again.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we ought to think of God even more often than we draw our breath; and if the expression is permissible, we ought to do nothing else.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yea, I am one of those who entirely approve that Word which bids us meditate day and night, and tell at eventide and morning and noon day, and praise the Lord at every time; or, to use Moses’ words, whether a man lie down, or rise up, or walk by the way, or whatever else he be doing—and by this recollection we are to be moulded to purity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So that it is not the continual remembrance of God that I would hinder, but only the talking about God; nor even that as in itself wrong, but only when unseasonable; nor all teaching, but only want of moderation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As of even honey repletion and satiety, though it be of honey, produce vomiting; and, as Solomon says and I think, there is a time for every thing, and that which is good ceases to be good if it be not done in a good way; just as a flower is quite out of season in winter, and just as a man’s dress does not become a woman, nor a woman’s a man; and as geometry is out of place in mourning, or tears at a carousal; shall we in this instance alone disregard the proper time, in a matter in which most of all due season should be respected?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Surely not, my friends and brethren (for I will still call you Brethren, though you do not behave like brothers).

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us not think so nor yet, like hot tempered and hard mouthed horses, throwing off our rider Reason, and casting away Reverence, that keeps us within due limits, run far away from the turning point, but let us philosophize within our proper bounds, and not be carried away into Egypt, nor be swept down into Assyria, nor sing the Lord’s song in a strange land, by which I mean before any kind of audience, strangers or kindred, hostile or friendly, kindly or the reverse, who watch what we do with over great care, and would like the spark of what is wrong in us to become a flame, and secretly kindle and fan it and raise it to heaven with their breath and make it higher than the Babylonian flame which burnt up every thing around it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For since their strength lies not in their own dogmas, they hunt for it in our weak points.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And therefore they apply themselves to our—shall I say “misfortunes” or “failings”?—like flies to wounds.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But let us at least be no longer ignorant of ourselves, or pay too little attention to the due order in these matters.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if it be impossible to put an end to the existing hostility, let us at least agree upon this, that we will utter Mysteries under our breath, and holy things in a holy manner, and we will not cast to ears profane that which may not be uttered, nor give evidence that we possess less gravity than those who worship demons, and serve shameful fables and deeds; for they would sooner give their blood to the uninitiated than certain words.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But let us recognize that as in dress and diet and laughter and demeanour there is a certain decorum, so there is also in speech and silence; since among so many titles and powers of God, we pay the highest honour to The Word.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let even our disputings then be kept within bounds.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why should a man who is a hostile listener to such words be allowed to hear about the Generation of God, or his creation, or how God was made out of things which had no existence, or of section and analysis and division?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why do we make our accusers judges?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why do we put swords into the hands of our enemies?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How, thinkest thou, or with what temper, will the arguments about such subjects be received by one who approves of adulteries, and corruption of children, and who worships the passions and cannot conceive of aught higher than the body…who till very lately set up gods for himself, and gods too who were noted for the vilest deeds?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Will it not first be from a material standpoint, shamefully and ignorantly, and in the sense to which he has been accustomed?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Will he not make thy Theology a defence for his own gods and passions?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if we ourselves wantonly misuse these words, it will be a long time before we shall persuade them to accept our philosophy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if they are in their own persons inventors of evil things, how should they refrain from grasping at such things when offered to them?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such results come to us from mutual contest.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such results follow to those who fight for the Word beyond what the Word approves; they are behaving like mad people, who set their own house on fire, or tear their own children, or disavow their own parents, taking them for strangers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But when we have put away from the conversation those who are strangers to it, and sent the great legion on its way to the abyss into the herd of swine, the next thing is to look to ourselves, and polish our theological self to beauty like a statue.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The first point to be considered is—What is this great rivalry of speech and endless talking?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What is this new disease of insatiability?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why have we tied our hands and armed our tongues?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We do not praise either hospitality, or brotherly love, or conjugal affection, or virginity; nor do we admire liberality to the poor, or the chanting of Psalms, or nightlong vigils, or tears.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We do not keep under the body by fasting, or go forth to God by prayer; nor do we subject the worse to the better—I mean the dust to the spirit—as they would do who form a just judgment of our composite nature; we do not make our life a preparation for death; nor do we make ourselves masters of our passions, mindful of our heavenly nobility; nor tame our anger when it swells and rages, nor our pride that bringeth to a fall, nor unreasonable grief, nor unchastened pleasure, nor meretricious laughter, nor undisciplined eyes, nor insatiable ears, nor excessive talk, nor absurd thoughts, nor aught of the occasions which the Evil One gets against us from sources within ourselves; bringing upon us the death that comes through the windows, as Holy Scripture saith; that is, through the senses.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nay we do the very opposite, and have given liberty to the passions of others, as kings give releases from service in honour of a victory, only on condition that they incline to our side, and make their assault upon God more boldly, or more impiously.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And we give them an evil reward for a thing which is not good, license of tongue for their impiety.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And yet, O talkative Dialectician, I will ask thee one small question, and answer thou me, as He saith to Job, Who through whirlwind and cloud giveth Divine admonitions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are there many mansions in God’s House, as thou hast heard, or only one?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of course you will admit that there are many, and not only one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now, are they all to be filled, or only some, and others not; so that some will be left empty, and will have been prepared to no purpose?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of course all will be filled, for nothing can be in vain which has been done by God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And can you tell me what you will consider this Mansion to be?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is it the rest and glory which is in store There for the Blessed, or something else?—No, not anything else.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Since then we are agreed upon this point, let us further examine another also.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is there any thing that procures these Mansions, as I think there is; or is there nothing?—Certainly there is—What is it?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is it not that there are various modes of conduct, and various purposes, one leading one way, another another way, according to the proportion of faith, and these we call Ways?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Must we, then, travel all, or some of these Ways…the same individual along them all, if that be possible; or, if not, along as many as may be; or else along some of them?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And even if this may not be, it would still be a great thing, at least as it appears to me, to travel excellently along even one.—“You are right in your conception.”—What then when you hear there is but One way, and that a narrow one, does the word seem to you to shew?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That there is but one on account of its excellence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is but one, even though it be split into many parts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And narrow because of its difficulties, and because it is trodden by few in comparison with the multitude of the adversaries, and of those who travel along the road of wickedness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “So I think too.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well, then, my good friend, since this is so, why do you, as though condemning our doctrine for a certain poverty, rush headlong down that one which leads through what you call arguments and speculations, but I frivolities and quackeries?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let Paul reprove you with those bitter reproaches, in which, after his list of the Gifts of Grace, he says, Are all Apostles?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are all Prophets? etc.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, be it so.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Lofty thou art, even beyond the lofty, even above the clouds, if thou wilt, a spectator of things invisible, a hearer of things unspeakable; one who hast ascended after Elias, and who after Moses hast been deemed worthy of the Vision of God, and after Paul hast been taken up into heaven; why dost thou mould the rest of thy fellows in one day into Saints, and ordain them Theologians, and as it were breathe into them instruction, and make them many councils of ignorant oracles?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why dost thou entangle those who are weaker in thy spider’s web, if it were something great and wise?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why dost thou stir up wasps’ nests against the Faith?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why dost thou suddenly spring a flood of dialectics upon us, as the fables of old did the Giants?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why hast thou collected all that is frivolous and unmanly among men, like a rabble, into one torrent, and having made them more effeminate by flattery, fashioned a new workshop, cleverly making a harvest for thyself out of their want of understanding?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Dost thou deny that this is so, and are the other matters of no account to thee?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Must thy tongue rule at any cost, and canst thou not restrain the birthpang of thy speech?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Thou mayest find many other honourable subjects for discussion.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To these turn this disease of thine with some advantage.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Attack the silence of Pythagoras, and the Orphic beans, and the novel brag about “The Master said.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Attack the ideas of Plato, and the transmigrations and courses of our souls, and the reminiscences, and the unlovely loves of the soul for lovely bodies.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Attack the atheism of Epicurus, and his atoms, and his unphilosophic pleasure; or Aristotle’s petty Providence, and his artificial system, and his discourses about the mortality of the soul, and the humanitarianism of his doctrine.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Attack the superciliousness of the Stoa, or the greed and vulgarity of the Cynic.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Attack the “Void and Full” (what nonsense), and all the details about the gods and the sacrifices and the idols and demons, whether beneficent or malignant, and all the tricks that people play with divination, evoking of gods, or of souls, and the power of the stars.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if these things seem to thee unworthy of discussion as petty and already often confuted, and thou wilt keep to thy line, and seek the satisfaction of thy ambition in it; then here too I will provide thee with broad paths.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Philosophize about the world or worlds; about matter; about soul; about natures endowed with reason, good or bad; about resurrection, about judgment, about reward, or the Sufferings of Christ.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in these subjects to hit the mark is not useless, and to miss it is not dangerous.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But with God we shall have converse, in this life only in a small degree; but a little later, it may be, more perfectly, in the Same, our Lord Jesus Christ, to Whom be glory for ever.
